Everyone will tell you Vite is faster. Almost no one will tell you why Webpack is still running in production at companies that could easily afford to leave it, what Vite quietly asks you to give up, or which of the benchmark numbers floating around the internet you should actually trust.
If you search “Vite vs Webpack” right now, you’ll get roughly the same article forty times. Webpack is the mature, configurable veteran. Vite is the fast, modern upstart. Pick Vite for new projects, keep Webpack if you’re stuck with it, the end. Somewhere in there you’ll usually find a chart claiming Vite’s dev server starts some implausibly precise number of times faster than Webpack’s.
Here’s the part that doesn’t make it into those posts: those numbers rarely agree with each other. While researching this piece, weekly npm download figures for Vite in mid-2026 ranged from roughly 20 million to over 140 million depending on which “comparison” site you read — often published within days of each other. That’s not a rounding error, that’s a sign a lot of this content is templated, unverified, and copy-pasted with the year changed. So before…
